details.description: Urban street-and-bridge scene in winter light. In the foreground are bicycle racks and several parked bikes on a cobblestone surface. People stand and walk near a low stone parapet and bollards covered with graffiti, suggesting a small plaza at the head of a bridge. A circular road sign indicates a 2.8‑ton weight limit. To the left stands a distinctive mid‑century/modernist corner apartment block in pastel peach and yellow tones, with stacked, sharply cantilevered balconies and slender vertical fins rising at the roofline; curved stair/shaft elements and ribbon-like window bands accent the façade. Behind it are older late‑19th/early‑20th‑century Mietskaserne-style buildings with tall windows and bay elements. Leafless trees frame the right side; classic Berlin-style street lamps are visible. The warm, low sun casts long shadows and highlights the façades.

entities.name: Admiralbrücke
entities.notes: Historic bridge over the Landwehrkanal and popular gathering spot in Kreuzberg; weight‑limit sign visible.

details.description: A street-level scene in Berlin featuring a distinctive pastel-orange residential building with stacked rounded balconies. The facade combines soft peach tones with horizontal window bands and glass-enclosed bays. Tall vertical metallic elements rise above the roofline, giving a modernist vertical emphasis. In the foreground, cobblestone pavement, metal bollards, and a row of bicycles are visible. A leafless, sprawling tree to the right casts intricate shadows, and pedestrians walk along the street, suggesting a lively urban neighborhood. Other surrounding buildings line the avenue, contributing to a dense, late-20th-century cityscape typical of Kreuzberg. Clear blue sky indicates daytime with bright, directional sunlight highlighting the building’s contours.

entities.name: Residential building with stacked curved balconies (Art Moderne)
entities.notes: Pastel orange-peach facade; rounded bay windows; horizontal window bands; vertical metal fin elements on the roof.

entities.name: Art Moderne / early 20th-century modernist architecture
entities.notes: Style inferred from form: curved balconies, vertical emphasis, and glass elements typical of Berlin modernist residential architecture.

details.description: Urban street scene in Kreuzberg, Berlin: people gather on a cobbled plaza beside a historic stone bridge with classic lanterns and a weight‑limit traffic sign (2.8 t). In warm afternoon light, a distinctive pastel yellow/orange corner apartment building with stacked cantilevered balconies and vertical fin-like roof elements stands at the edge of the square. Leafless winter trees frame the view; several bicycles are parked along railings and bollards. The setting appears to be around the Admiralbrücke area by the Landwehr Canal.

details.description: An urban, postwar mid-20th-century modernist apartment building dominates the left side of a cobblestone square in Berlin's Kreuzberg district near Böcklerstraße. The peach-pink façade features stacked rounded bay window sections on multiple floors and balconies with glass railings. A distinctive corner is accentuated by vertical pale-yellow metal fins, with slender metallic elements protruding along the upper façade, giving the building a sculptural vertical emphasis. Bare deciduous trees frame the scene, and pedestrians in winter clothing stroll along the street beside lampposts. In the foreground, bicycles are parked along a low barrier, contributing to the lively residential atmosphere.

entities.name: Mid-20th-century modernist apartment building
entities.notes: Peach-pink façade, rounded bay balconies, glass railings; corner with vertical pale-yellow fins; slender metal elements on roof.

entities.name: Postwar modernist architecture
entities.notes: Describes the overall style visible in the building and its vertical emphasis.

entities.name: Cobblestone square with bicycles
entities.notes: Foreground area with parked bicycles and low barrier; urban pedestrian setting.

details.description: A street scene in Berlin-Kreuzberg featuring a distinctive postmodern residential building on the left, designed by architects Hinrich and Inken Baller. The building, part of the IBA 1987 exhibition, is characterized by its peach and yellow façade, curved balconies, floor-to-ceiling windows, and a unique roofline with vertical metal spikes. To the right, large bare trees stretch their branches across a clear blue sky, contrasting with the architecture. The ground is paved with traditional cobblestones. In the foreground, numerous bicycles are parked near metal bollards, and pedestrians can be seen walking in the distance. A traffic sign indicating a 2.8t weight limit is visible, suggesting the proximity to a bridge. The lighting indicates a sunny day with long shadows.

entities.name: Fraenkelufer 38
entities.notes: Residential building designed by Hinrich and Inken Baller, completed around 1984-1985 for IBA 1987.

details.description: A street scene in Berlin featuring a distinctive postmodern residential building with a peach-colored facade, large windows, and sharp, angular roofline elements. The building, designed by Hinrich and Inken Baller, stands at a corner overlooking a cobblestone public space. In the foreground, several bicycles are parked in metal racks on a paved area. People are seen walking across the square. To the right, a large, leafless tree frames the scene, and traditional 19th-century Berlin apartment buildings (Altbau) are visible in the background. A traffic sign indicating a 2.8-ton weight limit is prominent. The lighting suggests late afternoon sun, casting long shadows and a warm glow on the structures.

details.description: Urban street corner with midrise residential buildings in winter light. The primary subject is a pale yellow/cream apartment block with a postwar/modernist character: flat roof, stucco facade, stacked balconies, and distinctive vertical brise-soleil fins and projecting canopies above the windows. Ground floor contains small retail spaces; some graffiti is visible near the corner. Behind and to the right stands an older, darker masonry/brick tenement typical of late 19th–early 20th century European ‘Altbau’ architecture. A cobblestone plaza or broad sidewalk occupies the foreground, lined with numerous parked bicycles and simple metal racks. Leafless street trees frame the scene. Street furniture and signs include a blue parking sign (with accessibility symbol) and a circular weight‑limit sign. Several pedestrians are present.

details.description: A European urban street scene dominated by a pastel-colored mid-20th-century residential building with Art Deco/Modernist influences. The building features a stepped central facade with orange-tan and pale yellow panels, vertical bays, and projecting balconies with glass railings. Ground-floor storefronts display large windows and awnings. In the foreground, a cobblestone street hosts a line of bicycles parked at racks and several pedestrians. Leafless trees arch over the street, their bare branches creating a network across the sky. The lighting suggests late morning to early afternoon on a clear day.

Ground level appears to have shopfronts. Many bicycles are parked at racks along a paved/cobblestone sidewalk. Several pedestrians in winter clothing are gathered near the intersection. Additional older-style buildings and some scaffolding are visible across the street.

details.description: A sunny European street scene showing a wide multi-story pastel-yellow/beige building with staggered balconies and vertical upper-story elements. In the foreground to the left, a large deciduous tree with bare branches dominates the frame. Ground-level storefronts with glass windows and awnings line the street, where bicycles are parked along racks and several pedestrians walk or stand near the shops. The street is paved with light-colored stones, and a blue parking sign and a circular traffic sign are visible in the background. The overall mood suggests a lively urban plaza or pedestrian area in a European city on a clear day.

details.description: A street-level view in an urban setting, likely Berlin, captured on a sunny winter day. The image is rotated 90 degrees clockwise. The scene features a distinctive postmodern residential building with a facade composed of yellow and orange sections, vertical white fins, and recessed balconies. The top of the building has an unfinished or industrial aesthetic with exposed metal elements. In the foreground, there is a wide cobblestone sidewalk with several parked bicycles locked to metal racks. A large bare tree with intricate branches dominates the upper left portion of the frame (when upright), casting shadows. The street level shows signs of urban grit, including heavy graffiti on the lower walls of the buildings and utility boxes. Pedestrians in winter clothing are walking along the sidewalk. Signage includes a '2,8t' weight limit sign and a parking zone sign ('P ZONE'). In the background, a traditional grey Altbau (old building) facade is visible behind the trees.

details.description: A street scene in the Kreuzberg district of Berlin, Germany, shown in a 90-degree counter-clockwise rotated orientation. The focal point is a postmodern residential building at the corner of Schlesische Straße and Falckensteinstraße, featuring a yellow and tan facade with white protruding balconies and a distinctive jagged roofline with vertical metal elements. This building, located at Schlesische Straße 1-4, was designed by architect Wilhelm Holzbauer for the International Building Exhibition (IBA) 1987. In the background, partially obscured by a large, leafless tree, is the famous 'Bonjour Tristesse' building designed by Álvaro Siza Vieira. The street level shows a cobblestone pavement, numerous parked bicycles, pedestrians, and German traffic signs, including a 2.8-ton weight limit and a parking zone sign. The lighting suggests late afternoon sun.

subject_location.name: Intersection of Schlesische Straße and Falckensteinstraße, Berlin

entities.name: Wohnhaus Schlesische Straße 1-4
entities.notes: Postmodern residential building designed by Wilhelm Holzbauer for IBA 87.

entities.name: Bonjour Tristesse (Wohnhaus am Schlesischen Tor)
entities.notes: Social housing building designed by Álvaro Siza Vieira, a landmark of IBA 87.

entities.name: IBA 87 (Internationale Bauausstellung Berlin 1987)
entities.notes: A major urban renewal and architectural exhibition in West Berlin.
